subtasks.description: Query SAM system to retrieve contract renewal dates, current license counts, and vendor information
Query consumption database to retrieve usage trends over the last 6-12 months (monthly or quarterly aggregation) and forecast trajectory

Reconcile SAM contract renewal dates with consumption trends; classify renewal risk (critical, high, medium, low) based on renewal window, usage growth, and cost trajectory; generate renewal recommendations
Generate and send renewal alert notifications to procurement team with risk classification, recommended action, and timeline

plan.cost_rationale: Four subtasks required: (1) SAM query (sql_gen, small) - straightforward single-source contract retrieval; (2) consumption trends (sql_gen, mid) - aggregation with historical windowing requires moderate complexity; (3) risk reconciliation (cross_recon, mid) - reconciles two sources (contracts + consumption) and includes risk classification and recommendations as part of reconciliation output; (4) notification dispatch (multitool_plan, small) - explicit multi-tool orchestration request (email/messaging to procurement). No separate policy_action needed as risk classification is integrated into cross_recon. Minimized to one specialist call per distinct data domain plus final action dispatch.

output.sql: SELECT i.contract_id, i.vendor_name, i.due_date, i.invoice_date, COUNT(DISTINCT c.license_id) as current_license_count, SUM(c.daily_active_seats) as total_active_seats, MAX(c.event_date) as last_consumption_date, SUM(c.amount_usd) as total_consumption_usd FROM invoice_facts i LEFT JOIN consumption_facts c ON i.vendor_name = c.publisher WHERE i.status IN ('open', 'pending', 'overdue') AND i.due_date >= CURRENT_DATE GROUP BY i.contract_id, i.vendor_name, i.due_date, i.invoice_date ORDER BY i.due_date ASC

WITH consumption_trends AS (SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', event_date)::DATE AS month, license_id, publisher, product, SUM(amount_usd) AS monthly_revenue, AVG(daily_active_seats) AS avg_seats, COUNT(DISTINCT user_id) AS unique_users FROM consumption_facts WHERE event_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '12 months' GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('month', event_date), license_id, publisher, product), trend_analysis AS (SELECT month, license_id, publisher, product, monthly_revenue, avg_seats, unique_users, LAG(monthly_revenue) OVER (PARTITION BY license_id ORDER BY month) AS prev_month_revenue, ROUND(((monthly_revenue - LAG(monthly_revenue) OVER (PARTITION BY license_id ORDER BY month)) / LAG(monthly_revenue) OVER (PARTITION BY license_id ORDER BY month) * 100)::NUMERIC, 2) AS mom_growth_pct FROM consumption_trends), renewal_risk AS (SELECT i.contract_id, i.vendor_name, i.invoice_date, i.due_date, i.amount_usd, ta.license_id, ta.publisher, ta.product, ta.monthly_revenue, ta.avg_seats, ta.unique_users, ta.mom_growth_pct, CASE WHEN ta.mom_growth_pct < -10 THEN 'HIGH_RISK' WHEN ta.mom_growth_pct BETWEEN -10 AND 0 THEN 'MEDIUM_RISK' WHEN ta.mom_growth_pct > 0 THEN 'LOW_RISK' ELSE 'UNKNOWN' END AS renewal_risk_level FROM trend_analysis ta LEFT JOIN invoice_facts i ON ta.license_id::TEXT = i.contract_id::TEXT AND i.due_date >= CURRENT_DATE AND i.status IN ('OPEN', 'PENDING') WHERE ta.month = (SELECT MAX(month) FROM consumption_trends)) SELECT contract_id, vendor_name, license_id, publisher, product, ROUND(monthly_revenue::NUMERIC, 2) AS last_month_consumption_usd, ROUND(avg_seats::NUMERIC, 2) AS avg_active_seats, unique_users, mom_growth_pct, renewal_risk_level, invoice_date, due_date, ROUND(amount_usd::NUMERIC, 2) AS invoice_amount FROM renewal_risk ORDER BY renewal_risk_level DESC, due_date ASC
